```python
# test_email_validation.py
import pytest

from your_module import is_valid_email  # replace "your_module" with the actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        "test@example.com",
        "user.name@example.com",
        "user_name@example.com",
        "user-name@example.com",
        "user+tag@example.com",
        "user%tag@example.com",
        "user123@example.co",
        "USER@example.COM",
        "user@example-domain.com",
        "user@example.subdomain.com",
        "a@b.co",
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_happy_path(email):
    assert is_valid_email(email) is True


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        "",
        "plainaddress",
        "missing-at-sign.com",
        "@example.com",
        "user@",
        "user@example",
        "user@example.",
        "user@example.c",
        "user@example.123",
        "user@.com",
        "user@com",
        "user name@example.com",
        "user@example com",
        "user@@example.com",
        "user@example..com",
        ".user@example.com",
        "user.@example.com",
        "user@-example.com",
        "user@example-.com",
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_invalid_formats(email):
    assert is_valid_email(email) is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        "test@example.com ",
        " test@example.com",
        "\ntest@example.com",
        "test@example.com\n",
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_rejects_surrounding_whitespace(email):
    assert is_valid_email(email) is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        None,
        123,
        12.34,
        [],
        {},
        b"test@example.com",
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_non_string_inputs_raise_type_error(email):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        is_valid_email(email)
```
